importance_sampling_1000.description: IS with 1000 particles, same model structure

linreg.note: GenMLX VIS is vectorized (1000 particles in one MLX graph)

hmm.note: HMM VIS 1000 particles, 50 timesteps, flat vectorized model

particle_scaling.note: GenMLX: 10x particles costs only 28% more time. JAX: 4.24x.

mcmc_5000_steps.description: MH with 5000 steps on linear regression
mcmc_5000_steps.note: Compiled MH with fused Metal execution

smc_hmm.note: GenMLX batched SMC leverages Metal GPU parallelism

hmc_funnel.note: HMC requires sequential leapfrog steps — interpreter overhead dominates

unique_genmlx_features.note: Features unique to GenMLX — no Gen.jl or JAX equivalent

genmlx_wins: Vectorized IS: 1.8-2.2x faster than Gen.jl on all models (LinReg, GMM, HMM)
Batched SMC: 1.07x faster than Gen.jl (Metal GPU parallelism)
Sublinear particle scaling: 10x particles costs 28% more time
Unique L3/L4 features: auto-conjugacy, compiled optimization, method selection
jax_wins: Bare JIT computation: 5x faster IS (XLA JIT vs Metal lazy graph)
genjax_comparison: GenMLX VIS linreg: 0.71ms vs GenJAX seq 1652ms (2327x faster), GenJAX vmap 15.3ms (21.5x faster)
GenMLX VIS GMM: 1.59ms vs GenJAX seq 6018ms (3786x faster)
GenJAX vmap closes gap significantly but GenMLX VIS still wins
summary.key_insight: GenMLX wins where parallelism matters (IS, SMC, compiled MH), loses where sequential steps dominate (HMC). The compilation ladder progressively closes the gap.
